title: ～でならない
titleEn: Indicates an uncontrollable emotion or feeling; can’t help but, overwhelmingly
structure: な-Adjective + でならない / い-Adjective (く) + てならない / Verb-て + ならない
structureEn: な-Adjective + でならない / い-Adjective (く) + てならない / Verb-て + ならない
explanation: Dùng để diễn tả cảm xúc tự nhiên dâng lên rất mạnh và khó kìm, thường trang trọng hơn てたまらない. Có thể hiểu là “vô cùng...”, “không khỏi...”. Cấu trúc: な-Adjective + でならない / い-Adjective (く) + てならない / Verb-て + ならない.
explanationEn: The ～でならない grammar pattern expresses a very strong, often involuntary feeling—e.g., 不思議でならない (it’s so mysterious I can’t help thinking about it), 残念でならない (I’m so disappointed), 心配でならない (I’m extremely worried)
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～でばかりはいられない
titleEn: Cannot afford to only do something; cannot always rely on it
structure: Verb-て form + ばかりはいられない / Noun + ばかりはいられない (with appropriate particles)
structureEn: Verb-て form + ばかりはいられない / Noun + ばかりはいられない (with appropriate particles)
explanation: Dùng khi nhận ra không thể cứ chỉ làm một việc, dựa vào một tình trạng hoặc ở mãi một trạng thái như trước; cần hành động hoặc thay đổi. Cấu trúc: Verb-て form + ばかりはいられない / Noun + ばかりはいられない (with appropriate particles).
explanationEn: The ～でばかりはいられない grammar point is used when one realizes they cannot keep doing only that one thing or cannot always rely on a certain situation. It implies the need to take other actions or responsibilities
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～ではないか
titleEn: Used to show a strong assertion or emphasis on one’s opinion; isnt it?, arent you?, right?
structure: Noun + ではないか / い-Adjective + ではないか / な-Adjective + ではないか / Verb-casual + ではないか
structureEn: Noun + ではないか / い-Adjective + ではないか / な-Adjective + ではないか / Verb-casual + ではないか
explanation: Dùng ở cuối câu để nhấn mạnh ý kiến hoặc đặt câu hỏi tu từ, hàm ý người nói cho rằng điều đó đúng. Có thể hiểu là “chẳng phải là... sao?”, “đúng không?”. Cấu trúc: Noun + ではないか / い-Adjective + ではないか / な-Adjective + ではないか / Verb-casual + ではないか.
explanationEn: The ～ではないか grammar point is used at the end of a sentence to make a rhetorical question or a strong, emphatic statement. It often translates as isnt it...? arent you...? or wouldn’t you say...?
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～てはならない
titleEn: Express prohibition; must not, cannot, should not
structure: Verb-て form + はならない
structureEn: Verb-て form + はならない
explanation: Dùng để cấm đoán mạnh hoặc nói một việc không được phép làm. Sắc thái trang trọng, nghiêm hơn てはいけない. Cấu trúc: Verb-て form + はならない.
explanationEn: The ～てはならない grammar point is used to express a strong prohibition. It is often more formal or strict and can be translated as must not, cannot, or should not
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～てまで
titleEn: Express the extent someone goes in order to achieve something; to the point of, even, so far as to
explanation: Dùng để nhấn mạnh mức độ sẵn sàng làm đến cả A để đạt mục đích B, đôi khi hàm ý người nói thấy hành động đó quá mức. Có thể hiểu là “đến mức...”, “thậm chí phải...”. Cấu trúc: Verb-て form + まで.
explanationEn: The ～てまで grammar point is used to convey the extent someone goes to achieve something, highlighting the action one takes or the sacrifice one makes for a certain purpose. It can be translated as to the point of, even, or so far as to in English. It is used with verbs, not adjectives or nouns
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～て当然だ
titleEn: Expresses that something is natural or expected
explanation: Dùng để nói một kết quả, phản ứng hoặc đánh giá là tự nhiên, hiển nhiên trong hoàn cảnh đó. Có thể hiểu là “... là đương nhiên”, “không có gì lạ khi...”. Cấu trúc: Verb-て form + 当然だ.
explanationEn: The ～て当然だ grammar point is used to express that something is natural, obvious, or expected under the circumstances. It can be translated as its natural that, as expected, or no wonder in English
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～というものだ
titleEn: Expressing a general rule or common sense
explanation: Dùng để khẳng định đó là lẽ thường, bản chất hoặc nhận định hợp lý về một việc. Sắc thái mạnh hơn giải thích thông thường, gần với “đó mới là...”, “đó là điều hiển nhiên”. Cấu trúc: Sentence + というものだ.
explanationEn: The ～というものだ grammar point is used to emphasize a general rule, a piece of common sense, or an indisputable fact. It often appears in comments or observations about something commonly accepted as true
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)
